abstract: Transfer data securely to connected accessories that you develop.
overview:
## Overview

Use the Accessory Transport Extension framework to securely transfer information to an accessory that you develop. First, establish a connection with your accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessorySetupKit]. Then, you can share Wi-Fi networks with your accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/WiFiInfrastructure], or forward iOS system notifications to your accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryNotifications].

## Share a Wi-Fi network with an accessory

Implement the [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension] protocol in an extension to share Wi-Fi networks with your accessory. The system calls your extension’s [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension/accept(sessionRequest:)] method when it’s ready to start. After accepting the session request, your extension connects to the accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessorySetupKit/ASAccessorySession] and delivers Wi-Fi network data using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/WiFiInfrastructure/WINetworkSharingProvider].

## Forward iOS system notifications to an accessory

Using the Accessory Transport Extension framework with [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryNotifications], your app can receive iOS system notifications to send alerts to people on a connected accessory that you develop. The workflow requires three extensions to maintain security and encapsulation. Implement [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryDataProvider] to receive and curate the content of a given notification. The system encrypts the notification data using keys you provide in your [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportSecurity] extension. Then, the system delivers the encrypted data to your [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension] for transmission to the accessory. Your transport extension sends the encrypted data to your accessory, but is unable to decipher the notification’s content.

# Accessory Transport Extension

**Framework**

Transfer data securely to connected accessories that you develop.

## Overview

Use the Accessory Transport Extension framework to securely transfer information to an accessory that you develop. First, establish a connection with your accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessorySetupKit]. Then, you can share Wi-Fi networks with your accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/WiFiInfrastructure], or forward iOS system notifications to your accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryNotifications].



## Share a Wi-Fi network with an accessory

Implement the [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension] protocol in an extension to share Wi-Fi networks with your accessory. The system calls your extension’s [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension/accept(sessionRequest:)] method when it’s ready to start. After accepting the session request, your extension connects to the accessory using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessorySetupKit/ASAccessorySession] and delivers Wi-Fi network data using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/WiFiInfrastructure/WINetworkSharingProvider].

## Forward iOS system notifications to an accessory

Using the Accessory Transport Extension framework with [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryNotifications], your app can receive iOS system notifications to send alerts to people on a connected accessory that you develop. The workflow requires three extensions to maintain security and encapsulation. Implement [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryDataProvider] to receive and curate the content of a given notification. The system encrypts the notification data using keys you provide in your [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportSecurity] extension. Then, the system delivers the encrypted data to your [doc://com.apple.AccessoryTransportExtension/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension] for transmission to the accessory. Your transport extension sends the encrypted data to your accessory, but is unable to decipher the notification’s content.

## Essentials

- **com.apple.developer.accessory-transport-extension**: A Boolean value that indicates whether your app can exchange sensitive information with a connected accessory.

## Wi-Fi network sharing

- **AccessoryTransportAppExtension**: A protocol for an extension that transmits data to an accessory you develop.
- **AccessoryTransportExtensionConfiguration**: An interface that enables you to configure and manage communication between your extension and the system.
- **AccessoryTransportSession**: A class that manages a transport session between the extension and the system.
- **Wi-Fi Infrastructure**: Share Wi-Fi network credentials securely between devices and connected accessories.

## Notification forwarding

- **Receiving iOS notifications on an accessory**: Create custom app extensions that manage notifications for your accessory.
- **AccessoryDataProvider**: A protocol for an extension that receives iOS system notifications and curates their data for your accessory.
- **AccessoryDataProviderConfiguration**: A protocol that configures and manages communication between the extension and the system.
- **AccessoryTransportSecurity**: A protocol for an extension that handles the cryptography of messages to your accessory.
- **AccessoryTransportSecurityConfiguration**: A protocol that configures and manages communication between your security extension and the system.
- **Accessory Notifications**: Receive forwarded iOS system notifications on an accessory that you develop.

## Data and sessions

- **AccessoryFeature**: A protocol that defines a capability for an accessory data provider extension.
- **AccessoryFeatureSession**: A protocol that manages a session for a specific feature capability.
- **AccessoryMessage**: A structure that represents a message to send to an accessory.
- **AccessorySecuritySession**: A class that manages a security session between the extension and the system.
- **AccessorySecurity**: Types of security events and cryptography operations.
